test_cases.test_case: Successful sign in using registered email and valid password
preconditions: User is registered with email 'user@example.com' and password 'P@ssw0rd1'
User is on the Login page
test_cases.expected_result: Flash message 'Signed in successfully.' is displayed and the user is redirected to the Accounts Overview page

test_cases.test_case: Successful sign in using registered username and valid password
preconditions: User is registered with username 'testuser' and password 'Strong#123'
User is on the Login page
test_cases.expected_result: Flash message 'Signed in successfully.' is displayed and the user is redirected to the Accounts Overview page

test_cases.test_case: Attempt sign in with incorrect password for existing user
preconditions: User is registered with email 'user@example.com' and password 'P@ssw0rd1'
User is on the Login page
test_cases.expected_result: Error message 'Incorrect email or password. Please try again.' is displayed; the Password field is cleared and the user remains on the Login page allowing another attempt

test_cases.test_case: Attempt sign in with an unregistered email
preconditions: User is on the Login page
test_cases.expected_result: Error message 'Incorrect email or password. Please try again.' is displayed; the Password field is cleared and the user remains on the Login page

test_cases.test_case: Submit with invalid email format
preconditions: User is on the Login page
test_cases.expected_result: Client-side validation prevents submission and displays an inline error like 'Please enter a valid email address.'; no authentication attempt is sent

test_cases.test_case: Submit with password shorter than minimum length
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 2. Enter 'Aa1!' (fewer than 8 characters) in the Password field
test_cases.expected_result: Client-side validation prevents submission and displays an inline error like 'Password must be at least 8 characters and include uppercase, lowercase, number, and special character.'; no authentication attempt is sent

test_cases.test_case: Submit password missing required character classes (no uppercase)
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 2. Enter 'p@ssw0rd!' (no uppercase letter) in the Password field
test_cases.expected_result: Client-side validation prevents submission and displays an inline error like 'Password must include uppercase, lowercase, number, and special character.'; no authentication attempt is sent

test_cases.test_case: Sign in with password at exact minimum length (8) meeting all complexity rules
preconditions: User is registered with email 'minpass@example.com' and password 'Aa1!aa11' (8 characters, includes uppercase, lowercase, number, special char)
User is on the Login page
test_cases.expected_result: Flash message 'Signed in successfully.' is displayed and the user is redirected to the Accounts Overview page

test_cases.test_case: Attempt sign in using SQL injection strings in input fields
preconditions: User is on the Login page
Registered users exist in the database
steps: 1. Enter "' OR '1'='1@example.com" in the Email/Username field
2. Enter "' OR '1'='1" in the Password field
test_cases.expected_result: System securely validates inputs; authentication fails and the generic error 'Incorrect email or password. Please try again.' is displayed; the Password field is cleared and no database compromise occurs

test_cases.test_case: Submit the form with Email/Username field empty
preconditions: User is on the Login page
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation displays 'Email or Username is required.' and submission is prevented; user remains on the Login page

test_cases.test_case: Submit the form with Password field empty
preconditions: User is on the Login page
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation displays 'Password is required.' and submission is prevented; user remains on the Login page

test_cases.test_case: Fail authentication once and verify the password field is cleared and user can immediately retry
preconditions: User is registered with email 'user@example.com' and password 'P@ssw0rd1'
User is on the Login page
steps: 3. Click 'Sign In' and observe the failure
4. Re-enter the correct password 'P@ssw0rd1' in the Password field
test_cases.expected_result: After step 3 the message 'Incorrect email or password. Please try again.' is displayed and the Password field is cleared; after step 5, with correct credentials, flash 'Signed in successfully.' appears and user is redirected to Accounts Overview

test_cases.test_case: Successful registration with all valid inputs
preconditions: User is on the Register page
test_cases.expected_result: Form accepts input; phone number displays as (217) 555-1234 and SSN displays as 123-45-6789; page shows 'Account created successfully — please sign in' and user is redirected to the Login page

test_cases.test_case: Successful registration with password at minimum length (8 characters)
preconditions: User is on the Register page
steps: 1. Fill all required fields with valid values
test_cases.expected_result: Registration succeeds; system shows 'Account created successfully — please sign in' and redirects to Login

test_cases.test_case: Successful registration with ZIP+4 format
preconditions: User is on the Register page
steps: 1. Fill all required fields with valid values
test_cases.expected_result: ZIP+4 accepted; registration succeeds with 'Account created successfully — please sign in' and redirect to Login

test_cases.test_case: Automatic formatting applied for Phone and SSN when digits are pasted
preconditions: User is on the Register page
steps: 3. Fill remaining required fields with valid values
test_cases.expected_result: Phone displays as (987) 654-3210 and SSN as 987-65-4321; registration succeeds and user is redirected to Login

test_cases.test_case: Submission fails when a required field (First Name) is empty
preconditions: User is on the Register page
steps: 2. Fill all other required fields with valid values
test_cases.expected_result: Form validation prevents submission; an inline error 'First Name is required' is displayed and user remains on Register page

test_cases.test_case: Invalid Username (not an email) is rejected
preconditions: User is on the Register page
steps: 1. Fill all required fields with valid values except Username
test_cases.expected_result: Inline error 'Enter a valid email address' is shown for Username; registration is not processed

test_cases.test_case: Phone Number with too few digits is rejected
preconditions: User is on the Register page
steps: 1. Fill all required fields with valid values except Phone Number
test_cases.expected_result: Inline error 'Enter a valid phone number (e.g., (123) 456-7890)' is displayed; submission blocked

test_cases.test_case: ZIP Code with letters is rejected
preconditions: User is on the Register page
steps: 1. Fill all required fields with valid values except ZIP Code
test_cases.expected_result: Inline error 'Enter a valid ZIP Code (12345 or 12345-6789)' is shown; registration not allowed

test_cases.test_case: SSN with wrong pattern is rejected
preconditions: User is on the Register page
steps: 1. Fill all required fields with valid values except SSN
test_cases.expected_result: Inline error 'Enter a valid SSN (e.g., 123-45-6789)' is displayed; submission blocked

test_cases.test_case: Password shorter than 8 characters is rejected
preconditions: User is on the Register page
steps: 1. Fill all required fields with valid values except Password
test_cases.expected_result: Inline error 'Password must be at least 8 characters' is shown; registration is not processed

test_cases.test_case: Confirm Password not matching Password is rejected
preconditions: User is on the Register page
steps: 1. Fill all required fields with valid values
test_cases.expected_result: Inline error 'Passwords do not match' is shown next to Confirm Password; submission blocked

test_cases.test_case: Leading and trailing spaces are trimmed from text fields on submission
preconditions: User is on the Register page
steps: 1. Enter First Name: '  Alice  ' (with leading/trailing spaces)
2. Enter other required fields with valid values (some with extra spaces)
test_cases.expected_result: System trims whitespace and accepts inputs if otherwise valid; registration succeeds and user is redirected to Login

test_cases.test_case: Very long names handled (e.g., 255 characters)
preconditions: User is on the Register page
steps: 3. Fill remaining required fields with valid values
test_cases.expected_result: Form either accepts and persists the long names or shows a clear validation message specifying maximum length; UI remains stable (no crash/overflow)

test_cases.test_case: Boundary ZIP codes (00000 and 99999) accepted if valid format
preconditions: User is on the Register page
steps: 1. Fill required fields with valid values using ZIP Code: 00000
test_cases.expected_result: Both 00000 and 99999 are treated as syntactically valid ZIP codes; registration succeeds if all other validations pass

test_cases.test_case: Pasting fully formatted Phone and SSN (with separators) is accepted and preserved
preconditions: User is on the Register page
steps: 1. Paste Phone Number as (415) 123-4567 into Phone field
2. Paste SSN as 321-54-9876 into SSN field
3. Fill remaining fields with valid values
test_cases.expected_result: System accepts formatted Phone and SSN without altering content; registration succeeds and user is redirected to Login

test_cases.test_case: Dashboard displays welcome message and accounts table with footer total
steps: 1. Navigate to the Accounts Overview page from the dashboard or main menu
2. Observe the page header and accounts table
test_cases.expected_result: A welcome message containing the user's full name is visible (e.g., 'Welcome John Smith!'). The accounts table is displayed with columns: Account Number, Account Type, Current Balance, Account Status, Open Date. A footer row is visible showing the total balance across all accounts. Table rows are present for each customer account.

test_cases.test_case: Account numbers are masked showing only the last 4 digits
preconditions: At least one account exists with a known account number ending in 5001
steps: 2. Locate the row for the account with the known account number
3. Inspect the Account Number column value
test_cases.expected_result: The account number is masked for security and displays only the last four digits, e.g., '****5001' (no full account number is visible).

test_cases.test_case: Rows are ordered by account creation date (earliest first)
preconditions: User has at least three accounts with distinct open dates
steps: 2. Note the Open Date values for the first, middle and last rows
3. Verify the ordering of rows by open date
test_cases.expected_result: Rows are ordered by Open Date ascending (earliest creation date appears in the first row and the latest in the last row).

test_cases.test_case: Active accounts show 'Active' status badge
preconditions: At least one account exists and is in Active status
steps: 2. Locate an account known to be Active
3. Inspect the Account Status column for that row
test_cases.expected_result: The Account Status column shows an 'Active' badge for the active account and the badge styling is visible as per design (clear label indicating Active).

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to access Accounts Overview when not logged in
steps: 1. Open the Accounts Overview URL directly in a new browser session without logging in
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the login page (or shown an authentication required message). Accounts Overview content is not displayed.

test_cases.test_case: Account full numbers are not exposed in the UI
preconditions: Tester has access to the full account numbers via backend/test data for verification
steps: 2. For each displayed account row, compare visible Account Number text with the known full account number
3. Verify no full account number digits beyond the last four are present in the page source or visible text
test_cases.expected_result: No account shows its unmasked full account number in the UI or page source. Only masked forms that reveal at most the last four digits are present. If any full account number appears, it is a failure.

test_cases.test_case: User with no accounts sees appropriate empty state and total zero
steps: 2. Observe the accounts table area and footer
test_cases.expected_result: Table displays a clear empty state (e.g., 'No accounts found' or empty table body). Footer displays a total balance of 0.00 (or equivalent formatted zero) and no account rows are present.

test_cases.test_case: Accounts with negative balances and cents sum correctly in footer
preconditions: User has multiple accounts including negative balances and balances with cents (e.g., -12.34, 100.56)
steps: 2. Note each account's Current Balance including sign and cents
3. Calculate the expected total by summing the individual balances to two decimal places
4. Compare the calculated total with the footer total displayed
test_cases.expected_result: Each account balance is displayed with correct sign and two-decimal formatting. The footer total equals the arithmetic sum of all displayed balances to two decimal places. Negative totals are displayed correctly (with a minus sign or parentheses) and formatting is consistent.

test_cases.test_case: Large number of accounts still shows correct ordering and accurate footer total
preconditions: User has a large number of accounts (e.g., 1000 test accounts) in test environment
steps: 2. Verify that the first and last visible rows correspond to the earliest and latest open dates respectively
3. Sample-check several rows across the table for ordering consistency
4. Optionally compute the sum of a subset of visible balances and compare with the corresponding portion of the footer total if pagination/virtualization is present
test_cases.expected_result: Page loads and renders the accounts list. The ordering by open date remains earliest-first for the visible rows. The footer shows the total balance which matches the sum of all accounts (for environments that display the full total regardless of paging). UI remains usable (no critical rendering errors).

test_cases.test_case: Open a Checking account with a valid deposit above minimum
preconditions: At least one funding account exists with a balance of at least $50
steps: 3. Enter 50 in the Initial Deposit Amount field
4. Select the funding account with at least $50 from the Funding Source Account dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Page shows 'Account opened successfully!' and redirects to Accounts Overview; the new Checking account appears and the funding account balance is reduced by $50

test_cases.test_case: Open a Savings account with a valid deposit above minimum
preconditions: At least one funding account exists with a balance of at least $150
steps: 3. Enter 150 in the Initial Deposit Amount field
4. Select the funding account with at least $150 from the Funding Source Account dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Page shows 'Account opened successfully!' and redirects to Accounts Overview; the new Savings account appears and the funding account balance is reduced by $150

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to open account without selecting account type
preconditions: A funding account exists with sufficient balance
steps: 2. Do not select Checking or Savings
3. Enter 50 in the Initial Deposit Amount field
4. Select a funding account from the Funding Source Account dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Real-time or submission validation shows an error like 'Please select an account type' and account is not created

test_cases.test_case: Enter an initial deposit below the minimum required for selected account
preconditions: A funding account exists with sufficient balance
steps: 3. Enter 20 in the Initial Deposit Amount field (below $25 minimum)
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error is displayed stating the minimum deposit (e.g., 'Deposit must be at least $25 for Checking'); account is not created

test_cases.test_case: Enter a non-numeric value in Initial Deposit Amount
steps: 3. Type 'abc' into the Initial Deposit Amount field
5. Observe real-time validation and attempt to click Open Account
test_cases.expected_result: Real-time validation shows an error like 'Enter a valid numeric amount' and submission is blocked; no account is created

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to open account when funding account has insufficient balance
preconditions: A funding account exists with a known balance of $75
steps: 3. Enter 100 in the Initial Deposit Amount field
4. Select the funding account that has only $75
test_cases.expected_result: Error message is displayed such as 'Insufficient funds in funding account' and account creation is prevented

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to open account without selecting a funding source
preconditions: At least one account could be used as funding but none selected
steps: 3. Enter 30 in the Initial Deposit Amount field
4. Do not select any option in the Funding Source Account dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error appears stating funding source is required (e.g., 'Please select a funding account'); account is not created

test_cases.test_case: Open a Savings account with the exact minimum deposit amount ($100)
preconditions: A funding account exists with at least $100
steps: 3. Enter 100 in the Initial Deposit Amount field
4. Select the funding account with at least $100
test_cases.expected_result: Page shows 'Account opened successfully!' and redirects to Accounts Overview; the new Savings account appears

test_cases.test_case: Open a Checking account with the exact minimum deposit amount ($25)
preconditions: A funding account exists with at least $25
steps: 3. Enter 25 in the Initial Deposit Amount field
4. Select the funding account with at least $25
test_cases.expected_result: Page shows 'Account opened successfully!' and redirects to Accounts Overview; the new Checking account appears

test_cases.test_case: Enter a valid deposit amount including cents (decimal) and open account
preconditions: A funding account exists with sufficient balance
steps: 3. Enter 25.50 in the Initial Deposit Amount field
4. Select a funding account with at least $25.50
test_cases.expected_result: Page shows 'Account opened successfully!' and redirects to Accounts Overview; new account appears and funding balance reduced by $25.50

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to open account with a very large deposit amount to test upper limits
preconditions: A funding account exists with a very large balance (or test environment supports large amounts)
steps: 3. Enter 1000000000 in the Initial Deposit Amount field
4. Select a funding account capable of covering the amount
test_cases.expected_result: System accepts the numeric amount if within application limits and shows 'Account opened successfully!' and redirects; if application limit is exceeded, a clear validation error is shown (e.g., 'Amount exceeds allowed maximum')

test_cases.test_case: Leave Initial Deposit Amount empty and observe real-time validation
steps: 3. Clear the Initial Deposit Amount field (leave empty)
test_cases.expected_result: Real-time validation indicates the deposit is required (e.g., 'Initial deposit is required') and submission is blocked

test_cases.test_case: Enter a deposit amount with more than two decimal places
preconditions: A funding account exists with sufficient balance
steps: 3. Enter 25.999 in the Initial Deposit Amount field
5. Click Open Account or observe real-time validation
test_cases.expected_result: System shows validation error or normalizes format (expected behaviour: validation error 'Enter a valid currency amount' or automatic rounding to two decimals with confirmation); account creation only proceeds if input is accepted or adjusted to a valid currency format

test_cases.test_case: Transfer funds between two internal ParaBank accounts successfully
preconditions: At least two Checking or Savings accounts exist with sufficient balance (e.g., Source balance >= transfer amount)
steps: 3. Enter 250.00 in the Transfer Amount field
4. Select <source account> from the Source Account dropdown
5. Select <destination account> from the Destination Account dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Page displays 'Transfer completed successfully.' with a transaction ID; Accounts Overview shows the source account balance decreased and the destination account balance increased by $250.00

test_cases.test_case: Transfer funds to an external account with matching account numbers
preconditions: At least one Checking or Savings account exists with sufficient balance
steps: 3. Enter 100.00 in the Transfer Amount field
4. Select <source account> from the Source Account dropdown
5. Enter 12345678 in the Account Number field
6. Enter 12345678 in the Confirm Account Number field
test_cases.expected_result: Page displays 'Transfer completed successfully.' with a transaction ID; source account balance decreased by $100.00 and transfer is recorded as an external transaction

test_cases.test_case: Attempt a transfer with amount exceeding source account balance
preconditions: A Checking or Savings account exists with a known balance (e.g., $500.00)
steps: 3. Enter 1000.00 in the Transfer Amount field (greater than the known balance)
4. Select the source and a destination account
test_cases.expected_result: An error message 'Insufficient funds' is displayed; no transfer is processed and balances remain unchanged

test_cases.test_case: External transfer with non-matching account numbers
preconditions: At least one Checking or Savings account exists with sufficient balance
steps: 3. Enter 50.00 in the Transfer Amount field
4. Select <source account> from the Source Account dropdown
5. Enter 11112222 in the Account Number field
6. Enter 22223333 in the Confirm Account Number field (does not match)
test_cases.expected_result: An error message 'Account numbers do not match.' is displayed; no transfer is processed

test_cases.test_case: Attempt transfer with empty Transfer Amount field
preconditions: At least one Checking or Savings account exists
steps: 2. Select either transfer type (Internal or External)
4. Select source and destination (or enter external account numbers if External selected)
test_cases.expected_result: A validation error is shown such as 'Please enter a valid transfer amount' and the transfer is not submitted

test_cases.test_case: Attempt transfer with zero or negative amount
preconditions: At least one Checking or Savings account exists
steps: 3. Enter 0.00 (or -10.00) in the Transfer Amount field
test_cases.expected_result: A validation error is displayed such as 'Transfer amount must be greater than 0' and the transfer is not processed

test_cases.test_case: User has no eligible source accounts (no Checking or Savings) and attempts transfer
preconditions: User has accounts, but none are Checking or Savings (e.g., only Loan or Credit Card accounts exist)
steps: 2. Observe the Source Account dropdown or transfer form
test_cases.expected_result: Source Account dropdown is empty or disabled and the UI shows an explanatory message such as 'No eligible source accounts available' or prevents submission; user cannot initiate a transfer

test_cases.test_case: Transfer an amount equal to the source account's entire balance
preconditions: A Checking or Savings account exists with a known balance (e.g., $500.00)
steps: 3. Enter 500.00 (the exact account balance) in the Transfer Amount field
4. Select the source and a destination account
test_cases.expected_result: If allowed by business rules, page shows 'Transfer completed successfully.' with a transaction ID; Accounts Overview shows the source account balance reduced to $0.00 and destination increased by $500.00. If disallowed, an 'Insufficient funds' or equivalent error is shown

test_cases.test_case: Source Account dropdown only lists Checking and Savings accounts
preconditions: User has multiple account types including Checking, Savings, Loan, Credit Card
test_cases.expected_result: Dropdown contains only the user's Checking and Savings accounts; Loan, Credit Card and other account types are not listed

test_cases.test_case: Transfer type toggle updates destination input fields correctly
preconditions: At least one eligible source account exists
steps: 2. Select 'My ParaBank Account' radio button and observe destination input (destination dropdown visible, external account fields hidden)
3. Select 'External Account' radio button and observe destination input (Account Number and Confirm Account Number fields visible, destination dropdown hidden)
4. Switch back to 'My ParaBank Account' and verify original state is restored
test_cases.expected_result: UI shows/hides destination controls according to transfer type selection: internal shows destination account dropdown, external shows account number and confirm account fields; no data leakage between fields and form validation applies correctly

test_cases.test_case: Submit a standard successful bill payment
preconditions: At least one source account exists with sufficient balance
steps: 1. Navigate to the Bill Payment page
2. Enter a valid Payee Name, Street Address, City, State, ZIP Code, and Phone Number
3. Enter a valid Payee Account Number and repeat the same number in Confirm Account Number
4. Enter a Payment Amount less than the source account balance
5. Select the Source Account from the dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Page displays 'Payment submitted successfully.' with a reference code; Accounts Overview (or account details) shows the source account balance decreased by the payment amount; the bill-payment form remains available/editable

test_cases.test_case: Submit a payment that equals the exact available balance (exact-fund payment)
preconditions: A source account exists with a known balance X
steps: 3. Enter Payee Account Number and Confirm Account Number matching
4. Enter Payment Amount exactly equal to the source account balance X
test_cases.expected_result: Page displays 'Payment submitted successfully.' with a reference code; the source account balance updates to zero (or to allowed minimum) and the payment posts; the form remains editable

test_cases.test_case: Submit payment using an alternative source account (e.g., Savings)
preconditions: Multiple source accounts exist (Checking and Savings) and the selected Savings account has sufficient funds
steps: 2. Fill in valid payee details and matching account numbers
3. Enter a valid Payment Amount within Savings account balance
4. Select the Savings account from Source Account dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Page displays 'Payment submitted successfully.' with a reference code; the Savings account balance decreases by the payment amount; the other accounts are unchanged

test_cases.test_case: Submit payment with ZIP+4 and formatted phone number accepted
preconditions: Source account with sufficient funds exists
steps: 2. Enter Payee details using a ZIP+4 format (e.g., 12345-6789) and a phone formatted as (123) 456-7890
3. Enter matching Payee Account Number and Confirm Account Number
4. Enter a valid Payment Amount and select Source Account
test_cases.expected_result: Page displays 'Payment submitted successfully.' with reference code; payment processes and source account balance updates accordingly; formatted ZIP and phone are accepted

test_cases.test_case: Attempt payment when Payee Account Number and Confirm Account Number do not match
preconditions: Source account exists with sufficient funds
steps: 2. Enter valid payee contact and address information
3. Enter a Payee Account Number and enter a different value in Confirm Account Number
4. Enter a valid Payment Amount and select Source Account
test_cases.expected_result: Inline error 'Account numbers do not match' (or equivalent) is displayed next to the account fields; payment is not submitted; the form remains editable for correction

test_cases.test_case: Attempt payment with amount exceeding the source account balance (insufficient funds)
preconditions: A source account exists with known balance Y
steps: 2. Enter valid payee details and matching account numbers
3. Enter a Payment Amount greater than Y
test_cases.expected_result: Inline error 'Insufficient funds' (or equivalent) is displayed; payment is not processed; source account balance remains unchanged; form remains editable

test_cases.test_case: Attempt payment with missing required field: Payee Name blank
steps: 2. Leave Payee Name blank and fill all other fields with valid values
3. Enter matching Payee Account Number and Confirm Account Number
4. Enter a valid Payment Amount and select Source Account
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error indicating Payee Name is required is displayed; payment is not submitted; form remains editable

test_cases.test_case: Attempt payment with non-numeric characters in Payment Amount
steps: 2. Fill in valid payee details and matching account numbers
3. Enter a Payment Amount containing letters or symbols (e.g., 'one hundred' or '100,00.00')
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error indicating invalid payment amount (must be numeric) is shown; payment not processed; form remains editable

test_cases.test_case: Attempt payment with zero or negative payment amount
steps: 2. Fill in valid payee details and matching account numbers
3. Enter Payment Amount 0 or a negative value (e.g., -50)
test_cases.expected_result: Inline error indicating payment amount must be greater than zero is displayed; payment not submitted; form remains editable

test_cases.test_case: Attempt payment without selecting a Source Account
preconditions: At least one account exists but user leaves Source Account unselected
steps: 2. Fill in valid payee details and matching account numbers
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error prompting selection of a Source Account is displayed; payment not processed; form remains editable

test_cases.test_case: Attempt payment with invalid ZIP code format (letters or too short)
steps: 2. Enter payee address with invalid ZIP (e.g., '12AB' or empty)
3. Fill other fields correctly including matching account numbers and valid amount
4. Select Source Account and click Pay
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error indicating invalid ZIP code format is displayed; payment not submitted; form remains editable

test_cases.test_case: Submit payment with a very long Payee Name (boundary length)
preconditions: Source account with sufficient funds exists
steps: 2. Enter a Payee Name at an extreme length (e.g., 500 characters) and valid values for other required fields including matching account numbers
3. Enter a valid Payment Amount and select Source Account
test_cases.expected_result: If accepted, page displays 'Payment submitted successfully.' with reference code and balance updates; if rejected, an inline error about Payee Name length appears and the form remains editable. The system must not crash

test_cases.test_case: Submit payment where Payee Account Number fields include leading/trailing spaces (trim behavior)
preconditions: Source account exists with sufficient funds
steps: 2. Enter Payee Account Number with leading/trailing spaces and enter the same value (including spaces) in Confirm Account Number
3. Fill other fields validly and enter Payment Amount within balance
4. Select Source Account and click Pay
test_cases.expected_result: System trims whitespace and either accepts matching account numbers (displays 'Payment submitted successfully.' and updates balance) or displays a clear validation message; form remains editable. No unexpected errors occur

test_cases.test_case: Submit a minimal valid payment amount ($0.01)
preconditions: Source account exists with at least $0.01
steps: 2. Fill in valid payee details and matching account numbers
4. Select Source Account and click Pay
test_cases.expected_result: Page displays 'Payment submitted successfully.' with a reference code; source account balance decreases by $0.01; form remains editable

test_cases.test_case: Approve Personal loan with valid amounts and sufficient collateral
preconditions: A Personal loan-eligible collateral account exists with a balance >= 20% of the requested loan amount
steps: 4. Enter 2000 in Down Payment (>= 10% and < loan amount)
5. Select the collateral account with balance >= 20% of 10000 from Collateral Account dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Page shows 'Loan approved and created successfully!' and displays new loan account details (loan id, terms, interest rate). Accounts/Loans overview shows the new loan record. (Note: mock system does not debit balances.)

test_cases.test_case: Approve Auto loan at minimum amount with exact 10% down payment and exact 20% collateral value
preconditions: An Auto loan-eligible collateral account exists with balance exactly 20% of $5,000 (i.e., $1,000)
Requested loan amount within Auto range ($5,000-$75,000)
steps: 5. Select the collateral account with balance $1,000 from Collateral Account dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Page shows 'Loan approved and created successfully!' with loan/account details. If credit engine denies (simulated 20% of cases), a denial message will appear — this test expects approval under normal simulated credit conditions.

test_cases.test_case: Approve Home loan at maximum amount with generous down payment and collateral
preconditions: A collateral account exists with balance >= 20% of $500,000
Requested loan amount within Home range ($50,000-$500,000)
steps: 4. Enter 100000 in Down Payment (>= 10% and < loan amount)
5. Select the collateral account with sufficient balance (>= 100,000) from Collateral Account dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Page shows 'Loan approved and created successfully!' and displays the created loan account details and terms. (Mock system: no balances are debited.)

test_cases.test_case: Reject loan request with amount below Personal minimum
test_cases.expected_result: The system displays a validation error indicating the loan amount is out of range (e.g., 'Loan amount must be between $1,000 and $50,000'). No loan is created.

test_cases.test_case: Reject loan request with amount above Auto maximum
steps: 3. Enter 100000 in Loan Amount (above Auto max $75,000)
test_cases.expected_result: The system displays a validation error indicating the loan amount is out of range (e.g., 'Loan amount must be between $5,000 and $75,000'). No loan is created.

test_cases.test_case: Reject loan when Down Payment is equal to or greater than Loan Amount
preconditions: Loan type selected supports given loan amount
steps: 4. Enter 10000 in Down Payment (equal to loan amount)
test_cases.expected_result: The system displays an error indicating Down Payment must be less than Loan Amount (e.g., 'Down payment must be less than the loan amount'). No loan is created.

test_cases.test_case: Reject loan when Down Payment is below required minimum (less than 10%)
steps: 5. Select a collateral account with sufficient balance
test_cases.expected_result: The system rejects the request with a message about minimum down payment (e.g., 'Minimum down payment is 10% of the loan amount') or credit engine denies due to insufficient down payment. No loan is created.

test_cases.test_case: Deny loan due to inadequate collateral value (collateral balance < 20% of loan amount)
preconditions: A collateral account exists but its balance is less than 20% of the requested loan amount
steps: 5. Select the collateral account with balance less than 20,000 from Collateral Account dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: The request is denied with a specific message like 'Inadequate collateral value' or a validation preventing submission. No loan is created.

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to submit without selecting a collateral account
steps: 3. Enter a valid Loan Amount and Down Payment
4. Leave Collateral Account dropdown unselected or at default 'Select account'
test_cases.expected_result: The system shows a validation error prompting selection of a collateral account (e.g., 'Please select a collateral account'). No loan is created.

test_cases.test_case: Request Personal loan at exact minimum with exact boundary down payment and collateral
preconditions: A collateral account exists with balance exactly 20% of $1,000 (i.e., $200)
steps: 5. Select the collateral account with balance $200 from Collateral Account dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: If credit engine approves (simulated 80% chance), page shows 'Loan approved and created successfully!' and displays loan details. If denied, a specific denial reason (e.g., 'Insufficient credit history') is displayed. No balances are debited in either case.

test_cases.test_case: Accept Loan Amounts with decimal/cents values
preconditions: Loan type chosen permits the entered decimal amount within allowed range
steps: 3. Enter 10000.50 in Loan Amount (decimal value within range)
4. Enter 1500.25 in Down Payment (>=10% and < loan amount)
5. Select a collateral account with sufficient balance
test_cases.expected_result: System accepts decimal amounts; either shows 'Loan approved and created successfully!' with loan details if approved by credit engine or shows specific denial message. Validation does not reject due to presence of cents.

test_cases.test_case: Reject non-numeric input in Loan Amount field
steps: 3. Enter 'ten thousand' or '10,000abc' in Loan Amount
4. Enter a valid numeric Down Payment
test_cases.expected_result: The system shows a validation error indicating loan amount must be numeric (e.g., 'Please enter a valid numeric loan amount'). No loan is created.

test_cases.test_case: Home loan at maximum boundary with exact 10% down payment and exact 20% collateral
preconditions: A collateral account exists with balance exactly 20% of $500,000 (i.e., $100,000)
steps: 5. Select the collateral account with balance $100,000 from Collateral Account dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: If credit engine approves, page shows 'Loan approved and created successfully!' with loan/account details. If denied, a specific denial reason is shown such as 'Insufficient credit history' or similar. The system validates boundary values correctly.

test_cases.test_case: Update a single contact field (Phone Number) with a valid value
preconditions: User is on the Customer Profile page with pre-filled fields
steps: 1. On the Customer Profile page, locate the Phone Number field
2. Replace the existing phone number with a valid 10-digit number (e.g. 5551234567)
test_cases.expected_result: An inline success message 'Profile updated successfully.' is shown; the page refreshes and the Phone Number displays the new value; no error banners or highlighted fields are present

test_cases.test_case: Update multiple contact fields with valid values
preconditions: User is on the Customer Profile page with pre-filled fields
steps: 1. Change First Name, Last Name, Street Address, City and State to valid new values
2. Enter a valid 5-digit ZIP code (e.g. 12345) and a valid phone number
test_cases.expected_result: An inline success message 'Profile updated successfully.' is shown; the page refreshes and all updated fields display the new values; no fields are highlighted and no error banner is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Submit the Update Profile form without changing any values
preconditions: User is on the Customer Profile page and fields are pre-filled
steps: 1. Confirm all pre-filled fields have valid values
2. Click the 'Update Profile' button without changing any field
test_cases.expected_result: An inline success message 'Profile updated successfully.' is shown; the page refreshes and the displayed data remains unchanged; no error banners or highlighted fields are present

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to update profile with First Name left blank
preconditions: User is on the Customer Profile page with pre-filled fields
steps: 1. Clear the First Name field so it is empty
test_cases.expected_result: An inline error banner is displayed and the First Name field is highlighted with an inline message indicating the field is required (e.g. 'First Name is required'); the profile is not updated and existing data remains unchanged

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to update profile with invalid ZIP code (non-numeric)
preconditions: User is on the Customer Profile page with pre-filled fields
steps: 1. Enter 'ABCDE' into the ZIP Code field
test_cases.expected_result: An inline error banner is displayed and the ZIP Code field is highlighted with a validation message indicating invalid ZIP format (e.g. 'ZIP code is invalid'); the profile is not updated

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to update profile with invalid Phone Number (too short and containing letters)
preconditions: User is on the Customer Profile page with pre-filled fields
steps: 1. Enter '12ab' into the Phone Number field
test_cases.expected_result: An inline error banner is displayed and the Phone Number field is highlighted with a validation message indicating invalid phone number format (e.g. 'Phone number is invalid'); the profile is not updated

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to update profile with an invalid State value (not in allowed list)
preconditions: User is on the Customer Profile page with pre-filled fields
steps: 1. Enter 'ZZZZ' (an invalid state code/name) into the State field
test_cases.expected_result: An inline error banner is displayed and the State field is highlighted with a validation message indicating invalid state selection; the profile is not updated

test_cases.test_case: Update Phone Number with common formatted value including parentheses and dashes
preconditions: User is on the Customer Profile page with pre-filled fields
steps: 1. Enter '(123) 456-7890' into the Phone Number field
test_cases.expected_result: An inline success message 'Profile updated successfully.' is shown and the page refreshes; the Phone Number displays the value in an acceptable format (either preserved or normalized) and no error is shown

test_cases.test_case: Update names using very long input at system boundary length
preconditions: User is on the Customer Profile page with pre-filled fields
A test value equal to the application's maximum expected length is available (e.g. 100 characters)
steps: 1. Enter a 100-character string into First Name and Last Name fields
test_cases.expected_result: If within allowed limits the inline success message 'Profile updated successfully.' is shown and the page refreshes showing the long names; if the length exceeds allowed maximum, an inline error banner appears and the name fields are highlighted with a length validation message; data is not updated in that case

test_cases.test_case: Enter a 9-digit ZIP code with hyphen (ZIP+4) and verify acceptance
preconditions: User is on the Customer Profile page with pre-filled fields
steps: 1. Enter '12345-6789' into the ZIP Code field
test_cases.expected_result: If the application supports ZIP+4, an inline success message 'Profile updated successfully.' is shown and the page refreshes showing the ZIP as entered or normalized; if not supported, an inline error banner and ZIP field highlight indicate invalid ZIP format; profile is updated only on success

test_cases.test_case: Request a Debit card with a complete shipping address and eligible account
preconditions: User has at least one linked account in good standing
steps: 1. In the Card Request card, select Card Type = Debit
2. Select an Account to Link that is in good standing
3. Enter a complete Shipping Address (street, city, state, ZIP, country)
test_cases.expected_result: Page shows message 'Card request submitted successfully.' with a tracking ID; a card-request ticket is opened in the system

test_cases.test_case: Request a Credit card for eligible account and verify confirmation
preconditions: User has at least one account eligible for a credit card
steps: 1. In the Card Request card, select Card Type = Credit
test_cases.expected_result: Confirmation message 'Card request submitted successfully.' with a tracking ID is displayed; linked account is validated and ticket created

test_cases.test_case: Update existing card controls: set valid spending limit, add travel notice, freeze card
preconditions: At least one active card exists and appears in Select Existing Card dropdown
Policy maximum spending limit is known
steps: 1. In the Card Controls card, select an existing Active card from the dropdown
2. Enter a New Spending Limit within the allowed policy (e.g., less than the max)
3. Enter a Travel Notice: Start Date = tomorrow, End Date = 7 days from tomorrow, add destinations
test_cases.expected_result: Page displays 'Card controls updated successfully.'; the card's spending limit, travel notice, and status reflect the changes; no validation errors are shown

test_cases.test_case: Reactivate a Frozen card to Active when allowed
preconditions: User has a card currently in Frozen status
steps: 1. In the Card Controls card, select the Frozen card from the dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Page displays 'Card controls updated successfully.'; the card status is updated to Active and the form remains editable

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to request a card with an incomplete shipping address
steps: 1. In the Card Request card, select Card Type and select an Account to Link
2. Enter a Shipping Address missing required fields (e.g., no ZIP or no street)
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error(s) highlight the missing address fields (e.g., 'Shipping address incomplete'); no ticket is created and the form remains editable

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to request a card for an account not in good standing
preconditions: User has at least one account in bad standing (e.g., blocked or delinquent)
steps: 1. In the Card Request card, select Card Type
2. Select the account that is not in good standing from Account to Link
test_cases.expected_result: An error message is displayed such as 'Selected account is not eligible for card requests'; no ticket is opened and the Card Request form remains editable

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to update controls with a spending limit above policy maximum
preconditions: At least one card exists and policy maximum spending limit is known
steps: 1. In the Card Controls card, select an existing card
2. Enter a New Spending Limit greater than the policy maximum
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears near the spending limit field stating limit exceeds policy (e.g., 'Limit exceeds allowed maximum'); the form remains editable and no update is applied

test_cases.test_case: Enter an invalid travel date range (end date before start date)
steps: 1. In the Card Controls card, select an existing card
2. Enter Travel Notice Start Date = 2026-06-10 and End Date = 2026-06-05 (end before start)
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears near the travel dates (e.g., 'End date must be on or after Start date'); update is rejected and form stays editable

test_cases.test_case: Enter non-numeric characters in the New Spending Limit field
steps: 1. In the Card Controls card, select an existing card
2. Enter invalid non-numeric input in New Spending Limit (e.g., 'five thousand')
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error indicates invalid numeric input (e.g., 'Enter a valid numeric amount'); the update is not applied and the field remains editable

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to update controls without selecting an existing card
preconditions: Select Existing Card dropdown default is 'Select' or empty
steps: 1. In the Card Controls card, leave Select Existing Card unselected
2. Enter a valid New Spending Limit and/or other fields
test_cases.expected_result: Inline or banner error indicates a card must be selected (e.g., 'Please select a card to update'); no update occurs and the form remains editable

test_cases.test_case: Request a card with a maximum-length shipping address
preconditions: System's maximum address field length is known
steps: 1. In the Card Request card, select Card Type and Account to Link
2. Enter a Shipping Address where one or more fields use the maximum allowed length (e.g., very long street name)
test_cases.expected_result: If within limits, 'Card request submitted successfully.' with tracking ID is shown; input is accepted without truncation or unexpected errors. If beyond limits, inline validation identifies the specific field length violation

test_cases.test_case: Request a card with shipping address containing special/unicode characters
steps: 1. In the Card Request card, select Card Type and Account to Link
2. Enter a Shipping Address containing special characters and Unicode (e.g., é, 漢字, emojis)
test_cases.expected_result: Address is accepted if allowed; 'Card request submitted successfully.' is shown. If characters are disallowed, an inline validation error is shown explaining allowed characters

test_cases.test_case: Set New Spending Limit equal to the policy maximum (boundary)
preconditions: Policy maximum spending limit is known
steps: 1. In the Card Controls card, select an existing card
2. Enter New Spending Limit equal to the policy maximum
test_cases.expected_result: Page displays 'Card controls updated successfully.'; the spending limit is set to the maximum without validation error

test_cases.test_case: Set New Spending Limit to zero to test boundary behavior
preconditions: Business rule about zero limits is known (allowed or disallowed)
steps: 1. In the Card Controls card, select an existing card
test_cases.expected_result: If zero is allowed, 'Card controls updated successfully.' and limit is set to 0; if disallowed, inline validation explains minimum allowed value and update is rejected

test_cases.test_case: Create a travel notice with start and end dates equal (single-day travel)
steps: 1. In the Card Controls card, select an existing card
2. Enter Travel Notice Start Date = 2026-06-10 and End Date = 2026-06-10
test_cases.expected_result: If single-day travel is allowed, 'Card controls updated successfully.' and travel notice is recorded; if not allowed, inline validation explains the rule

test_cases.test_case: Create a travel notice with a very long list of destinations
steps: 1. In the Card Controls card, select an existing card
3. Enter a long list of destinations (many entries or a long string) in the Destinations field
test_cases.expected_result: If allowed, 'Card controls updated successfully.' and all destinations are saved or truncated according to policy; if not allowed, inline validation appears indicating maximum destinations or character limit

test_cases.test_case: Execute a Buy trade for an existing fund with sufficient buying power
preconditions: Portfolio shows fund holdings and cash/buying power in at least one funding account
Fund symbol 'ABC' exists and price is known
Funding account has sufficient buying power for the purchase
steps: 3. Enter 'ABC' in Fund Symbol and select the matching autocomplete result
5. Select the funding account with sufficient buying power from the Funding Account dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Page shows 'Trade executed successfully.' with an order ID; Portfolio Snapshot updates to reflect the new holdings and cash/buying power reduced by the purchase amount

test_cases.test_case: Execute a Sell trade for an owned fund with sufficient share balance
preconditions: Portfolio contains at least one fund 'XYZ' with >= 50 shares
steps: 3. Enter 'XYZ' in Fund Symbol and select the matching autocomplete result
5. Select the destination/funding account from the dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Page shows 'Trade executed successfully.' with an order ID; Portfolio Snapshot decreases 'XYZ' holdings by 20 shares and cash/destination account balance increases accordingly

test_cases.test_case: Create a monthly recurring investment plan with valid future start date and sufficient funding
preconditions: Funding account has at least the minimum contribution available
steps: 1. Navigate to Investments > Recurring Investment Plan
2. Enter 'DEF' in Fund Symbol and select the suggestion
3. Enter Contribution Amount meeting the minimum (e.g., $50)
5. Choose a Start Date at least one week in the future
6. Select the Funding Account with sufficient balance
test_cases.expected_result: Page shows 'Plan created successfully.' and the plan appears in the user's scheduled plans list with correct symbol, amount, frequency and start date

test_cases.test_case: View portfolio snapshot as a read-only summary of holdings and unrealised gains/losses
preconditions: Account has at least one fund holding
test_cases.expected_result: Portfolio Snapshot displays current fund holdings, market value and unrealised gain or loss; fields are read-only and no edit controls are present

test_cases.test_case: Attempt a trade with a non-existent fund symbol
preconditions: No fund with symbol 'ZZZNOT' exists in the system
steps: 3. Type 'ZZZNOT' in Fund Symbol and attempt to submit (do not select any autocomplete suggestion)
4. Enter a valid Quantity (e.g., 1) and select a funding account
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation displays an error such as 'Symbol not found' or similar and the trade is not executed

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to execute a trade with zero or negative quantity
steps: 3. Enter 'ABC' in Fund Symbol and select suggestion
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation displays 'Quantity must be greater than zero'; trade is not executed

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to sell more shares than are owned
steps: 3. Enter 'LMN' in Fund Symbol and select suggestion
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation displays an error such as 'Insufficient share balance' and the trade is not executed

test_cases.test_case: Create a recurring plan with a start date in the past
steps: 1. Navigate to Investments > Recurring Investment Plan
2. Enter 'GHI' in Fund Symbol and select suggestion
5. Choose a Start Date in the past (e.g., yesterday)
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation highlights Start Date with message 'Start date must be in the future' (or similar) and plan is not created

test_cases.test_case: Create a recurring plan with Start Date = tomorrow (boundary future date)
preconditions: Funding account has adequate balance for initial contribution
steps: 1. Navigate to Investments > Recurring Investment Plan
2. Enter 'JKL' in Fund Symbol and select suggestion
3. Enter Contribution Amount equal to minimum allowed
test_cases.expected_result: Page shows 'Plan created successfully.' and the scheduled plan lists the start date as tomorrow; no start-date validation error is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Execute a Buy trade when funding account balance equals the exact purchase cost (no leftover buying power)
preconditions: Fund symbol 'MNO' exists with known price
Funding account cash = exact price * desired quantity
steps: 3. Enter 'MNO' in Fund Symbol and select suggestion
4. Enter Quantity that makes total cost equal to funding account balance
5. Select the funding account with exact available buying power
test_cases.expected_result: Page shows 'Trade executed successfully.' with an order ID; funding account balance becomes zero and Portfolio Snapshot shows the purchased holdings

test_cases.test_case: Execute a Buy trade using a very small fractional quantity (minimal positive quantity)
preconditions: Fund symbol 'PQR' exists and system supports fractional shares or minimal quantity granularity
Funding account has sufficient buying power for the fractional purchase
steps: 3. Enter 'PQR' in Fund Symbol and select suggestion
4. Enter a very small positive Quantity (e.g., 0.0001 or smallest supported fractional unit)
test_cases.expected_result: If fractional shares are supported: Page shows 'Trade executed successfully.' with an order ID and Portfolio Snapshot reflects the fractional holding; if fractional shares are not supported: inline validation displays a clear message (e.g., 'Quantity must be an integer' or 'Minimum quantity is X') and trade is not executed

test_cases.test_case: Generate monthly statement using month-and-year selector
preconditions: At least one account exists with transactions for the selected month
steps: 2. In the Generate Statement form select month-and-year mode
3. Choose <month-year> (e.g. May 2024) in the Statement Period control
test_cases.expected_result: Page shows 'Statement generated successfully.' and the statement view lists transactions for the selected month; account statement reflects correct start/end dates corresponding to the selected month

test_cases.test_case: Generate statement for a custom date range
preconditions: Selected account has transactions within the custom date range
steps: 2. In the Generate Statement form choose custom date range mode
3. Enter a valid Start Date and End Date where Start Date < End Date
test_cases.expected_result: Page shows 'Statement generated successfully.' and the statement view lists transactions between the provided dates only

test_cases.test_case: Enable paperless statements and save a valid email address
steps: 2. In the e-Statement Preference form check the 'Paperless statements' checkbox
3. Enter a valid email address (e.g. user@example.com) in the Email Address field
test_cases.expected_result: Page shows 'e-Statement preference updated.' and the preference persists (checkbox checked and email saved) when revisiting the Statements page

test_cases.test_case: Generate statement for an account with no transactions returns an empty statement
preconditions: Selected account exists and has zero transactions in the requested period
steps: 2. Select a month-or custom date range that yields no transactions
3. Select the account with no transactions from the Account dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Page shows 'Statement generated successfully.' and the statement view indicates no transactions for the period (e.g. 'No transactions found' or empty transactions table) without errors

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to generate statement with End Date before Start Date
steps: 2. In the Generate Statement form choose custom date range mode
3. Enter an End Date that is earlier than the Start Date
test_cases.expected_result: The system prevents generation and displays a validation error near the date fields (no 'Statement generated successfully.' message) explaining that the date range is invalid

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to generate statement without selecting an account
steps: 2. Choose any valid Statement Period (month or custom range)
3. Leave the Account dropdown unselected or set to default empty value
test_cases.expected_result: The system prevents generation and displays an error or inline validation prompting the user to select an account; no 'Statement generated successfully.' message appears

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to save e-statement preference with invalid email formats
steps: 3. Enter invalid email examples one at a time (e.g. 'plainaddress', 'user@', 'user@.com') in the Email Address field
test_cases.expected_result: The system rejects each invalid email, highlights the Email Address field and displays guidance about valid email format; no 'e-Statement preference updated.' message is shown

test_cases.test_case: Generate statement when Start Date equals End Date (single-day statement)
preconditions: Selected account may have transactions on that specific date
steps: 2. In the Generate Statement form choose custom date range mode
3. Enter the same date for Start Date and End Date
test_cases.expected_result: Page shows 'Statement generated successfully.' and the statement view lists only transactions from that single date (or shows 'No transactions found' if none exist)

test_cases.test_case: Save e-statement preference with a very long but valid email address
steps: 3. Enter a very long but valid email address near the system's expected max length (e.g. 254 characters) in the Email Address field
test_cases.expected_result: If the system accepts the address, it shows 'e-Statement preference updated.' and persists the long email; if there's a length limit, the Email Address field is highlighted with guidance explaining the maximum allowed length

test_cases.test_case: Simulate backend failure during statement generation to verify error message
preconditions: Mechanism to simulate backend/service failure is available (test environment)
steps: 2. Choose any valid Statement Period and select an Account
3. Configure backend to fail statement generation (simulate service outage or 500 response)
test_cases.expected_result: The page shows 'Unable to generate statement — please try again later.' and no partial statement is displayed; the UI remains stable and allows retrying the action

test_cases.test_case: Change password with valid current password and strong new password
preconditions: Collapsible Security Settings panel is accessible
steps: 2. If the change password panel is collapsed, expand it
4. Enter a strong password (e.g. 'Str0ng!Passw0rd') in New Password
5. Enter the same strong password in Confirm New Password
test_cases.expected_result: Page displays 'Password changed successfully.'; fields are cleared; credentials are updated so the user can log in with the new password and not with the old one

test_cases.test_case: Open collapsed panel then change password successfully
preconditions: Change password panel is initially collapsed
steps: 2. Verify the change password form is inside a collapsed panel
3. Click the panel header to expand the form
4. Fill Current Password, New Password and Confirm New Password with valid values
test_cases.expected_result: Panel expands to reveal the form; after submission the page shows 'Password changed successfully.' and the form is reset

test_cases.test_case: Attempt change with incorrect current password
preconditions: User does NOT know or enters incorrect current password
steps: 1. Navigate to Security Settings and expand the change password panel if necessary
2. Enter an incorrect value in Current Password
3. Enter a valid strong password in New Password and Confirm New Password
test_cases.expected_result: An error message such as 'Current password is incorrect' is displayed; Current Password field is highlighted as invalid; password is not changed

test_cases.test_case: New password and confirmation do not match
steps: 3. Enter a valid strong password in New Password
4. Enter a different value in Confirm New Password
test_cases.expected_result: An error message such as 'New password and confirmation do not match' is displayed; Confirm New Password field is highlighted; password is not changed

test_cases.test_case: New password fails strong-password policy (too weak)
preconditions: Application enforces a strong-password policy (e.g., minimum complexity)
steps: 3. Enter a weak password (e.g. 'password123') in New Password and Confirm New Password
test_cases.expected_result: A validation error such as 'Password does not meet strength requirements' is shown; New Password (and possibly Confirm) field(s) are highlighted; password is not changed

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to submit with Current Password field empty
steps: 3. Enter valid values in New Password and Confirm New Password
test_cases.expected_result: A validation error such as 'Current Password is required' is displayed; Current Password field is highlighted; password is not changed

test_cases.test_case: New password identical to current password
steps: 2. Enter the current password in Current Password
3. Enter the same value in New Password and Confirm New Password
test_cases.expected_result: A validation message such as 'New password must be different from current password' is displayed (or strong-policy message); appropriate fields are highlighted; password is not changed

test_cases.test_case: New password exceeds maximum allowed length
preconditions: Maximum password length is defined by the system
steps: 3. Enter an excessively long string (e.g. 256 characters) in New Password and Confirm New Password
test_cases.expected_result: A validation error such as 'Password is too long' is displayed or the input is truncated and an error shown; New Password (and Confirm) field(s) are highlighted; password is not changed

test_cases.test_case: Confirm New Password left empty
steps: 3. Enter a valid strong password in New Password
test_cases.expected_result: A validation error such as 'Please confirm your new password' is displayed; Confirm New Password field is highlighted; password is not changed

test_cases.test_case: Change password with new password at exact minimum length
preconditions: System minimum password length is known (assume 8 characters) and policy otherwise satisfied
steps: 3. Enter a new password that meets the strong-password requirements and is exactly the minimum length (e.g. 8-char meeting complexity) in New Password and Confirm New Password
test_cases.expected_result: If the password meets the policy (including minimum length), 'Password changed successfully.' is displayed and credentials updated; otherwise a clear policy error is shown indicating which requirement failed

test_cases.test_case: Change password containing Unicode characters and surrounding spaces
preconditions: System supports Unicode in passwords (or behavior to be verified)
steps: 3. Enter a new password that includes Unicode characters and leading/trailing spaces (e.g. '  PässwØrd!  ') in New Password and the identical value in Confirm New Password
test_cases.expected_result: If allowed, 'Password changed successfully.' is displayed and the exact password (including spaces and Unicode) is accepted for subsequent login; if not allowed, a clear validation error is shown indicating unsupported characters or trimming rules

test_cases.test_case: Send secure message with required fields only
steps: 1. On Support Center, select the Secure Message form
4. Enter a meaningful message in Message Body
test_cases.expected_result: Page displays 'Message sent successfully.' and shows a ticket ID; message appears in user's secure messages inbox or confirmations area

test_cases.test_case: Send secure message with a valid PDF attachment
preconditions: Have a valid PDF file available for upload (e.g., statement.pdf)
steps: 2. Enter 'Attached statement for review' in Subject
4. Enter message text in Message Body
test_cases.expected_result: Page displays 'Message sent successfully.' with a ticket ID; attached file is listed on the sent message record

test_cases.test_case: Request callback for the next business day with prefilled phone number
preconditions: User profile has a valid phone number prefilled
steps: 2. Select a Reason for Call (e.g., 'Account Inquiry')
3. Choose a Preferred Date that is the next business day
5. Confirm the pre-filled Phone Number or leave it unchanged
test_cases.expected_result: Page displays 'Callback request submitted.' and an email confirmation is sent to the user's registered email; callback appears in user's request history

test_cases.test_case: Request callback with edited valid phone number and chosen time window
steps: 3. Choose Preferred Date that is at least the next business day
5. Edit the Phone Number field to a valid format (e.g., '(123) 456-7890')
test_cases.expected_result: Page displays 'Callback request submitted.' and an email confirmation is received; the edited phone number is stored for the request

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to send secure message without a Message Body
steps: 2. Enter 'Forgot to include body' in Subject
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error shown near Message Body: 'Message body is required.'; message is not sent and no ticket ID is generated

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to send secure message with Subject exceeding maximum length
preconditions: Subject maximum length validation exists (e.g., 255 characters)
steps: 2. Enter a Subject string longer than allowed (e.g., 256+ characters)
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error is displayed near Subject (e.g., 'Subject cannot exceed 255 characters'); message is not sent

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to attach an invalid file type to secure message
preconditions: Have an .exe file available for upload
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation near Attachment shows 'Unsupported file type' or similar; upload is rejected and message is not sent

test_cases.test_case: Request callback with Preferred Date equal to today (not allowed)
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error shown near Preferred Date: 'Please select a date at least the next business day.'; callback is not submitted

test_cases.test_case: Request callback with invalid phone number format
steps: 3. Choose Preferred Date at least next business day
5. Edit Phone Number to an invalid value (e.g., '12345')
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation near Phone Number displays 'Enter a valid phone number' or format guidance; callback is not submitted

test_cases.test_case: Request callback with Preferred Date on a weekend (non-business day)
preconditions: Selected date is a Saturday or Sunday
steps: 3. Choose Preferred Date that falls on a weekend
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error near Preferred Date: 'Selected date must be a business day (at least the next business day)'; callback is not submitted

test_cases.test_case: Send secure message with Subject exactly at maximum allowed length (boundary)
preconditions: System enforces a Subject maximum length (assumed 255 characters)
steps: 2. Enter a Subject exactly 255 characters long
test_cases.expected_result: Message is accepted and 'Message sent successfully.' is shown with a ticket ID (boundary accepted)

test_cases.test_case: Send secure message with minimal Subject length (1 character) and no attachment
test_cases.expected_result: Message is accepted and 'Message sent successfully.' is displayed with a ticket ID

test_cases.test_case: Request callback when today is Friday and chosen date is next Monday (next business day calculation)
steps: 3. Choose Preferred Date equal to upcoming Monday (next business day)
test_cases.expected_result: Request is accepted and 'Callback request submitted.' is displayed; email confirmation is sent and the requested date is Monday (system correctly skips weekend)

test_cases.test_case: Request callback using international phone number in E.164 format
steps: 3. Choose a Preferred Date at least the next business day
5. Edit Phone Number to an international E.164 format (e.g., '+441234567890')
test_cases.expected_result: Request is accepted and 'Callback request submitted.' is displayed; email confirmation is sent and international phone number is stored for the request
